title: Claim Old Age Contributory Pension
description: Claim a pension from the National Insurance Office once you reach pension age.
summary: The Old Age Contributory Pension is a monthly payment from the National Insurance Office for people who have reached pension age and have paid enough National Insurance contributions during their working life.
Full pension age in Barbados is 67. You can also choose to claim from age 60 at a reduced rate, but only if you have stopped working. The reduction stays for as long as you receive the pension.

Whether you get the full or reduced rate depends on the number of contributions you have paid. The National Insurance Office will look at your record when you apply and confirm what you are eligible for.
Once you are receiving the pension, you have to send in a Life Certificate every June and December so payments keep coming. Most people are paid by direct deposit – see the related services for both.

items: you are between 60 and 66, are no longer working and choose to claim early at a reduced rate
you have paid the minimum number of NIS contributions
your date of birth and address
the bank or credit union account where you want your pension paid

items.name: Marriage certificate
items.note: Only if your surname is different from your birth certificate.

items.name: Bank or credit union account header

fees: There is no fee to claim.
how_long: A first decision usually takes 6 to 12 weeks. Payments are then made every month.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed Claim for Old Age Contributory Pension form to the National Insurance Office on Culloden Road.

options.heading: If you live overseas
content: Contact the National Insurance Office. They will tell you how to claim from where you live.

edge_cases.heading: If your claim is approved
content: You will get a letter telling you how much you will be paid and when. Most people are paid by direct deposit – see ‘Set up direct deposit for NIS payments’.
You will also need to send a Life Certificate every June and December to keep your pension active.

related.title: Set up direct deposit for NIS payments

assumptions: Pension age of 67 is the current statutory age. It has changed in the past and could change again. ‘Reduced rate from age 60’ is the long-standing rule but the exact percentage is not on the form.
‘A first decision usually takes 6 to 12 weeks’ is an estimate.

The list of documents (national ID, birth certificate, marriage certificate, bank header, payslips) is based on common NIS practice. The form lists relevant sections but not a full document checklist.
